title: Substack vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Moosend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which suits your needs best.
intro: Substack and Moosend serve different email marketing needs. Substack is built for individual writers monetizing newsletters directly, while Moosend is designed for small businesses seeking comprehensive automation and unlimited sending. Understanding their distinct purposes will help you choose the right platform.
choose_a_if: You're an independent writer wanting to monetize your audience directly
You prefer a simple, distraction-free publishing platform
You need unlimited subscribers without paying upfront fees
choose_b_if: You're a small business needing advanced automation and marketing workflows
You want unlimited email sending at a low cost
You need landing pages, A/B testing, and detailed analytics

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing newsletters, while Moosend is superior for businesses needing marketing automation and advanced features. Your choice depends on your primary goal: direct audience monetization or comprehensive email marketing.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Moosend?
faq.a: Substack is technically free with unlimited subscribers, taking only a 10% cut of paid subscriptions. Moosend is free up to 1,000 subscribers, then starts at $9/month. For small lists, Moosend's free tier is cheaper; for larger audiences, Substack's no-upfront-cost model wins.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Moosend and import it into Substack, though the platforms have different architectures. Note that Substack is designed primarily for newsletters, while Moosend handles broader email marketing needs, so some features won't transfer directly.
